/specification/overview/: mission, the two-channel catalog, and the knowledge framing.
/specification/schema-format/: how a schema declares its tools through main and handlers.
/specification/parameters/: how a parameter places a value and validates it before the call.
/specification/shared-lists/: versioned value sets that schemas reference instead of duplicating.
/specification/output-schema/: how a route declares its expected response shape.
/specification/security/: the trust boundary that keeps schema handlers off the network and filesystem.
/specification/agents/: how an agent bundles tools, a model, and a prompt into one composition.
/specification/tasks/: the reserved, forward-looking area for long-running asynchronous operations.
/specification/migration/: how to migrate schemas between FlowMCP versions.
/specification/validation-rules/: the wayfinder mapping each rule family to its home page.
/specification/tests/: executable examples that document tools and capture real responses.
/specification/preload/: how a tool declares that its response is safe to cache.
/specification/prompt-architecture/: the two-tier prompt system for provider and agent workflows.
/specification/resources/: local SQLite and Markdown data access alongside network tools.
/specification/skills/: reusable instruction sets an agent can load and follow.
/specification/catalog/: the registry.json manifest that groups schemas, lists, and agents.
/specification/id-schema/: the canonical namespace/type/name identifier for every primitive.
/specification/selections/: a named bundle of primitives an agent activates together.
/specification/prefill/: placeholder substitution and pre-executed tool calls in skill content.
/specification/mcp-integration/: the per-tool meta block and its mapping to MCP annotations.
/specification/validation-strategy/: the deterministic and probabilistic layers behind the A-F grade.
/specification/schema-lifecycle/: the six stages a schema travels from idea to production.
/specification/scoring-protocol/: the JSON formats exchanged between the CLI and an external grader.
/specification/license-and-tos/: the three-layer model linking code, provider ToS, and data licenses.
/specification/philosophy/: the worldview and guiding principles behind FlowMCP.
/grading/overview/: how FlowMCP schemas and selections are evaluated and graded.
/grading/default-journey/: how a schema enters the corpus and why maximal endpoint coverage is the default.
/grading/eligibility/: what is allowed to be part of a gradable schema before scoring begins.
/grading/tos/: how Terms-of-Service links are checked as a due-diligence score, never a hard gate.
/grading/phases-single/: the six provider-side areas that grade one schema without group context.
/grading/phases-selection/: the five selection-side areas that grade a curated group of namespaces.
/grading/determinism-and-tier/: the two axes that decide reproducibility and the highest grade a dimension can reach.
/grading/scoring-vs-grading/: why evidencing a single test stays separate from deriving a letter grade.
/grading/grading-model/: the grading entry data model, its veto power, and tier trim.
/grading/security-and-development/: the security and development checks that carry high categorical-veto affinity.
/grading/domain-knowledge/: how a selection's About Resource carries the group's domain knowledge.
/grading/about-convention/: the reserved About markdown Resource that describes what a namespace does.
/grading/personas-contract/: how a grading entry references one of the four base personas and a lens.
/grading/skills/: how namespace skills and leveled selection skills are graded differently.
/grading/kanban-data-contract/: the superseded phase-status contract, now replaced by the index.json rollup.
/grading/versioning-axes/: how primitives are versioned by filename timestamp and bound by a canonical hash.
/grading/selection-lockfile/: the neutral selection definition and the frozen member-pin lock snapshot.
/grading/scope-whitelist/: which FlowMCP constructs are gradable and the public-only data principle.
/grading/flywheel-loop/: the self-reinforcing import, grade, improve, export round-trip.
/grading/folder-layout/: the three top-level folders and timestamp-first naming grammar of the grading island.
/grading/entry-point-prompt/: the first prompt that anchors a grading run to persona, schema, mode, and version.
/grading/pre-conditions/: the universal rule blocking aggregate checks until every member schema is stable.
/grading/workbench-island/: the walled-off working directory that never overwrites a shipped source.
/grading/index-json/: the one rollup file per namespace and selection carrying status and grade.
/grading/selection-aggregate/: the eleventh area that grades a selection as a whole and gates Grade A.
/grading/harness-and-goal/: the completion-condition harness that drives non-deterministic grading turn by turn.
/grading/monitoring-track/: the deterministic GitHub board projecting each namespace's grading state.
/best-practice/overview/: the advisory track of recommendations and learnings for writing good schemas.
/best-practice/readable-interface/: designing the surface for the calling model with readable enums, names, and handler phases.
/best-practice/reference-data/: maintaining recurring lists and value formats once and inheriting them everywhere.
/best-practice/load-and-scale/: choosing the load and storage strategy by data size and query ability.
/best-practice/geo-conventions/: the established geo defaults that keep schemas interoperable and avoid silent mistakes.
/best-practice/correctness-license/: assuming nothing, faking nothing, and documenting data provenance and rights.
